D1 Optics L3 Apply Reference

Planck's Blackbody Radiation Curves

heat a blackbody and watch its spectrum brighten, peak and shift toward blue

A blackbody's emission depends only on its temperature. The Planck law gives the spectral radiance at every wavelength — a curve that rises and shifts blueward as the body heats.

Slide the temperature from a dim ember to a blue-white star. The curve is the Planck spectrum (visible band highlighted); the swatch shows the colour the body would glow.
